items.title: Kakadu National Park airport thrown lifeline to avoid bulldozers

children.content: Jabiru Airport had been slated for demolition in October this year, but after months of uncertainty, a new operator has emerged to take over.

gallery.altText: The sign of the Jabiru sign.

thumbnail.altText: The sign of the Jabiru sign.

items.title: Police charge man, 20, with murder over 26-year-old's death in Arnhem Land

children.content: Northern Territory police have charged a 20-year-old man with the murder of a 26-year-old man in Milingimbi last week.

image.altText: NT Police insignia on mud-splattered car door.

items.title: Report reveals nation's best and worst attempts to tackle illicit tobacco

children.content: Smoking is one of the leading causes of death in the Northern Territory. Now, the jurisdiction has been ranked the weakest in the country for how it tackles cheap, illicit tobacco.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, inspecting the packets on cigarettes in her hands.

items.title: Communities 'in the middle of nowhere' are scared they'll run out of fuel

children.content: Remote communities are calling for assurance on fuel security in the face of potential shortages, along with financial assistance as already-high fuel and food prices continue to climb.

gallery.altText: Alastair King stands at the checkout register of a small grocery store, speaking to the worker behind the counter.

thumbnail.altText: Alastair King stands at the checkout register of a small grocery store, speaking to the worker behind the counter.

avatar.alt: Jane Bardon in a blazer looking at the camera.

items.title: Al Kuwait ships cattle out of Darwin after weeks anchored near Fremantle

children.content: Anchored off Fremantle for weeks because the war in Iran had stopped the live sheep trade to the Middle East, the world's largest live export vessel has ventured north to Darwin.

image.altText: MV Al Kuwait at the Darwin Port

items.title: NT councils flag cuts to street lights, pool hours as result of power prices

children.content: Power price hikes are expected to be passed onto local councils by the Northern Territory government in the coming month, with Darwin's lord mayor warning some community services could be cut as a result.

image.altText: A sign outside an old building reads: City of Darwin.

items.title: Hospital staff using ChatGPT to calculate medication doses, nurses claim

children.content: Whistleblowers say understaffing at the Northern Territory's busiest hospital is forcing nurses to take on unsafe patient loads, and some are teaching themselves medical procedures via YouTube.

gallery.altText: A nurse in uniform walking through the corridor of a hospital, facing away from the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A nurse in uniform walking through the corridor of a hospital, facing away from the camera.

items.title: Think you have ADHD? Here's what getting assessed looks like

children.content: Who can diagnose ADHD, what a proper assessment should include, and the red flags to look out for.

articles.title: The neighbourhoods where ADHD rates are skyrocketing

children.content: ADHD diagnoses in adults have exploded across Australia since 2017. For the first time, we have the data that shows where it is — and isn't — happening.

image.altText: A search bar saying "Enter your area" is superimposed on a map of Australia against a blue-tinged backdrop of medication.

gallery.altText: A close up of a person marking a page on a clipboard, sitting across from a person clasping their hands.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a person marking a page on a clipboard, sitting across from a person clasping their hands.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith reports to police as NSW premier denies 'special treatment'

children.content: Ben Roberts-Smith has been seen reporting to police for the first time since he was released on bail after being charged with the war crime of murder.

image.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith is tall, wears sunglasses and a tank top while walking.

items.title: Rebel Wilson accused of defaming actor with sexual harassment claim

children.content: Hollywood star Rebel Wilson is being sued in the Federal Court for defamation by one of the lead actor's in her directorial debut film, The Deb.

image.altText: Rebel Wilson arrives at the Federal Court of Australia

items.title: 'Boy are we glad to see you': Diver describes rescue after 20 hours lost at sea

children.content: Stu Fillman was rescued alongside his dive instructor after being carried away from a dive company boat in rough swell.

gallery.altText: Mr Fillman has been left with lacerations to his neck.

thumbnail.altText: Mr Fillman has been left with lacerations to his neck.

items.title: Prospector couldn't believe his luck when he found rare australite

children.content: The object formed when a meteorite slammed into Earth more than 800,000 years ago.

image.altText: a small, round, black, slightly pitted rock held between man's fingers

items.title: Fishers make 'surprising' discovery of artificial leg on the beach

children.content: The prosthetic leg has been handed to police after being discovered by a couple while they were fishing on a NSW Mid North Coast beach.

image.altText: prosthetic leg pictured on the beach

items.title: Driver stood down after schoolboy dragged by bus

children.content: Authorities are investigating how a 12-year-old became trapped in a bus door before being dragged hundreds of metres down a suburban street in Melbourne.

image.altText: CCTV footage of a boy dragged by a schoolbus

gallery.altText: CCTV footage of a boy dragged by a schoolbus

thumbnail.altText: CCTV footage of a boy dragged by a schoolbus

items.title: Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed

children.content: The AFL appeals board has quashed Zak Butters' conviction for abusive language against umpire Nick Foot, saying a Tribunal member listening in while driving his car for part of the original hearing consitutes a miscarriage of justice. Follow live.

image.altText: AFL star midfielder Zak Butters.

items.title: Shane Flanagan's sacking is the same old story for St George Illawarra

children.content: St George Illawarra might have sacked coach Shane Flanagan amid a record losing streak, but such struggles have become sadly familiar to the joint venture.

image.altText: A group of rugby league players look on after conceding a try.

items.title: Tempted to order in? Try these quick recipes instead

children.content: From frittata to one-pot pasta, these speedy dinners are perfect for busy evenings and worth adding to your regular rotation.

gallery.altText: A patterned plate of spaghetti with prawns, lemon, chilli and garlic by Julia Busuttil Nishimura. An easy spring meal.

thumbnail.altText: A patterned plate of spaghetti with prawns, lemon, chilli and garlic by Julia Busuttil Nishimura. An easy spring meal.

items.title: Quiz: Can you tame our tricky history questions?

children.content: Is two-up actually illegal in Australia? And what movie beat Crocodile Dundee to the top of the box office in 1986?

image.altText: A still of Paul Hogan holding a crocodile from the 1986 film Crocodile Dundee

items.title: 'I had no-one to talk to': The 92yo trying to save his dying language

children.content: Peter is the last living speaker of the First Nations language Thiinma. When he met linguist Rosie, they used music to help keep his knowledge alive, and formed a beautiful friendship along the way.

image.altText: Peter stands on stage at a microphone with bright lights behind him, wearing an akubra, shirt and bolo tie

items.title: 'Hard to break the habit': Why free public transport isn't enough to shift from cars

children.content: Free public transport is a popular policy, but researchers say it's not enough to convince drivers to leave the car at home.

gallery.altText: A train on a city railway.

thumbnail.altText: A train on a city railway.

items.title: The world is on the cusp of an 'era of extreme scarcity'. Enter Vladimir Putin

children.content: With fears hunger could hit record levels, Vladimir Putin and Russia are poised to capitalise — financially and diplomatically.

image.altText: Vladimir Putin sits at a desk with a map in front of him in the Kremlin

items.title: Australia faced the same dilemma as China in 2004 but made the wrong call

children.content: While the rest of us count the cost in higher fuel costs, higher inflation and a weaker economy,  China — the actual winner of the war — will be enjoying a surge of global interest in its solar panels, wind turbines and electric vehicles.

image.altText: A man in a red hard-hat stands in a field of massive solar panels speaking to someone out of frame

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Milk, bread prices to increase within weeks due to war in Iran

children.content: Farmers ask for a 30-cent-per-litre price rise for milk as fuel and fertiliser prices surge.

image.altText: A woman shopping in a grocery store aisle.

items.title: 'Australia's most expensive soccer pitch' project blows out to $20m

children.content: Five years ago the conversion of a washout-prone natural field to synthetic should've cost $3.3 million. Project changes and a legal dispute with the old contractor has blown out the price.

image.altText: a football clubhouse surrounded by temporary fencing

items.title: What Megan Burslem and Jeremy Fernandez can reveal about ABC Classic 100 in Concert

children.content: The ABC Classic 100 in Concert brings Australia's most popular classical music, as chosen by audiences, from the radio to the stage.

gallery.altText: Jeremy Fernandez and Megan Burslem smile and sit back to back on a piano stool in front of a grand piano on a concert stage.

thumbnail.altText: Jeremy Fernandez and Megan Burslem smile and sit back to back on a piano stool in front of a grand piano on a concert stage.

items.title: Woman tells jury she lied after allegedly seeing mum prepare fatal smoothie

children.content: Maree Mavis Crabtree is fighting three charges at a trial in the Supreme Court in Brisbane, including the murder and attempted murder of her son Jonathan Crabtree.

image.altText: A middle aged woman wearing dark sunglasses and a black tshirt.

items.title: Inquest opens into police employee's shooting death at hands of 15yo in 2015

children.content: A coroner will examine what authorities knew about 15-year-old gunman Farhad Jabar in the lead-up to the attack.

image.altText: a woman holding a funeral flyer

items.title: Two-thirds of Australians leave private information open to cybercriminals

children.content: New research suggests Australians are leaving themselves vulnerable to cybercrime through their public social media accounts.

items.title: 'Privilege of my life': ACT Greens leader Shane Rattenbury quitting politics

children.content: The leader of the ACT Greens and former attorney-general Shane Rattenbury has announced he is leaving politics after more than 17 years in the Legislative Assembly.

image.altText: Shane speaks behind the podium and microphones.

items.title: Australia's biggest AI factory planned for remote WA

children.content: A huge artificial intelligence data centre capable of training models like ChatGPT is planned for WA's remote north at a cost of billons of dollars.

image.altText: A data centre

items.title: Grieving mother of Belgian backpacker warns hikers not to trust phone maps

children.content: Backpacker Celine Cremer set off on a short walk with only a phone to guide her — and vanished. More than two years later, her family has an urgent message for other travellers.

image.altText: Ariane Mathieu, Celine Cremer's mother sits in a home space wearing a red sweater, a serious facial expression

items.title: This country music singer's tour costs have got her rethinking her career

children.content: Adele Oliver admits being a country music artist has "never been a great career choice", but she says things are worse than ever, with her last gig costing her money due to the escalating price of fuel.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair stands with a guitar strapped to her body in a home.

items.title: Blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people

children.content: From today, Australian Red Cross Lifeblood will use gender-neutral assessments for blood and platelets donations.

items.title: Haas out of Origin as fellow Broncos prop hospitalised

children.content: Brisbane's injury crisis escalates, with Payne Haas to miss six to eight weeks and fellow prop Corey Jensen in hospital with a blood clot.

items.title: Man charged over alleged plot to import $80m cocaine haul into Australia

children.content: A New South Wales man has faced court after allegedly attempting to import 200 kilograms of cocaine into Australia.
